text: After we enter the chest in the fourth intercostal space, we divided lima in the same intercostal space and skeletonite lima under direct vision up to its origin.
We did this procedure with peripheral cardiopulmonary bypass, aortic cross clamping and cold blood cardioplegia.
We put the tape around the standing aorta.
This helped us to expose the aorta when we did proximal anastomosis and when we put the cannula for blood cardioplegia.
We put transthoracic clamp through the second intercostal space and we cross clamp the aorta under direct vision.
With the empty and rested heart it was easier to dissect the adhesions around the aneurysm
And after that we continued with the coronary artery bypass grafting.
To improve the exposure of coronary vessels and left ventricle aneurysm, we put separate tapes around left pulmonary veins.
and around inferior vena cava.
This helped us to bring all the targets closer to the surgeon.
After this exposure maneuvers, the distance between the skin and coronary vessels was less than 6 cm.
All knots were tied with our fingers.
We started with PDA anastomosis, and the last anastomosis was lima toledi.
Then we started left ventricle aneurysm repair.
In this case endoventricular circular parsed repair technique was performed.
The incision was made in the anterior wall of the left ventricle.
We removed all the traumatic mass from the left ventricle
and then cut out all the remnants of the left ventricle wall.
In this case, we decided that the mitral valve wasn't suitable to repair.
The anterior leaflet was divided in A2 segment and mitral valve replacement was performed with the preservation of both mitral valve leaflets.
First, we put all stitches in the mitral valve prosthesis and then around mitral valve annulus.
We had a nice exposure of the mitral lobe prosthesis and tied all knots with our fingers.
Then left ventricle aneurysm repair was continued.
We put a circular stitch to restore the left ventricle cavity and use a separate running suture to fix the Dekron patch inside.
Left ventricle wall was closed with several layers of continuous polypropylene sutures reinforced with pericardial strips.
Aorta was unclamped and proximal anastomosis was performed with the side biting clamped.
and sutures was also tied with fingers.
Post-op echo confirmed good mitral valve prosthesis function.
The experience with routine minimally invasive coronary artery bypass grafting gives the possibility to successfully and safely perform more complex procedures through the same incision.
